picks.rationale: The New York Knicks enter Game 6 on a two-game winning streak, including a dominant 29-point victory in Game 5 against the Hawks, showcasing strong recent form. The Atlanta Hawks, conversely, are on a two-game losing streak and have a 4-6 record in their last 10 games, indicating a struggle for consistency. While Josh Hart is questionable for the Knicks, insiders expect him to play, and the Hawks are without Jock Landale, further impacting their frontcourt depth. With the Knicks leading the series 3-2 and aiming to close it out, their motivation and recent performance suggest they are well-positioned to cover the spread on the road.

The Boston Celtics boast a clean injury report, entering Game 6 with their full rotation available for the first time in the series, which is a significant advantage. In contrast, Joel Embiid for the 76ers is probable but still recovering from appendectomy surgery and a recent knee bump, suggesting he's not at 100%, while Tyrese Maxey is also playing through a finger injury. Boston has demonstrated its capability to dominate Philadelphia, securing two prior wins in the series by 32 and 8 points. With the Celtics leading 3-2 and motivated to close out the series on the road to avoid a Game 7, their health and depth should allow them to cover the spread.

The Minnesota Timberwolves are severely hampered by injuries, with key players Anthony Edwards (knee) and Donte DiVincenzo (Achilles) ruled out for the remainder of the series, and Bones Hyland and Ayo Dosunmu questionable. This extensive injury list significantly weakens Minnesota's backcourt and overall offensive firepower. The Denver Nuggets, despite Aaron Gordon being questionable, secured a decisive 12-point victory in Game 5, demonstrating their ability to exploit the Timberwolves' depleted roster. With the Nuggets facing elimination and their core players like Nikola Jokic and Jamal Murray performing at an elite level, they are highly motivated to force a Game 7. The substantial injury advantage favors Denver to cover the -6.5 spread.

The Knicks' offense has shown its scoring potential, erupting for 126 points in Game 5, with Jalen Brunson leading the charge with 39 points. The Hawks have also contributed to higher-scoring games in the series, posting 109 and 107 points in their two victories. Atlanta's defense has been vulnerable, allowing 126 points in Game 5 and averaging 114.9 points allowed in their last 10 games. With the Hawks facing elimination at home, they are expected to play with urgency and push the pace, which should lead to more offensive possessions and a higher total score, pushing the game over 213.5.

Joel Embiid is listed as probable for Game 6 and is expected to play, having delivered a crucial 33-point performance in Game 5 to keep the 76ers' season alive. He is the primary offensive engine for Philadelphia, averaging 29.5 points per game in the playoffs despite playing through injury. In a must-win elimination game at home, the 76ers will heavily rely on Embiid to carry the scoring load, as he demonstrated his ability to dominate inside even when his perimeter shot was off in Game 5. Given his importance and recent scoring output in a high-stakes environment, Embiid is poised to exceed 29.5 points.

The Minnesota Wild are at home with a 3-2 series lead, aiming to close out the series against a significantly injured Dallas Stars team. Dallas is missing key forwards Roope Hintz and Arttu Hyry, which severely impacts their offensive depth and ability to match lines. Minnesota's power play has been revitalized by Mats Zuccarello's return, and their goaltender Jesper Wallstedt has outperformed Jake Oettinger in the series. Historically, road teams facing elimination in the playoffs win less than 45% of the time, further favoring the Wild to secure the win at home.

The Edmonton Oilers forced Game 6 with a dominant 4-1 victory in Game 5, showcasing their offensive firepower led by Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl. The Anaheim Ducks' defense has been a major weakness, allowing four or more goals in four of the five games in this series, and their defensive metrics were among the worst in the league at the end of the regular season. With their backs against the wall, the Oilers' high-powered offense is poised to exploit Anaheim's defensive struggles and extend the series.

Despite the total holding steady at 5.5, the Over has seen strong public support, indicating expectations for a higher-scoring affair. The Minnesota Wild have been the more dangerous offensive team in the series, averaging 3.5 goals per game, and their power play has improved with Mats Zuccarello back in the lineup. Dallas, facing elimination and struggling for 5-on-5 scoring, will be desperate to generate offense, which could lead to a more open game with increased scoring opportunities for both sides.

This series has consistently produced high-scoring games, with Anaheim winning Game 3 (7-4) and Game 4 (4-3 OT), and Edmonton winning Game 5 (4-1). The total has seen upward movement, reflecting the market's expectation of continued offensive output. Anaheim's defensive struggles, allowing four or more goals in four of the five games, combined with Edmonton's potent offense fighting for survival, suggest a game ripe for goals.

picks.pick: Leo Carlsson Over 3.5 Shots on Goal
picks.rationale: Anaheim Ducks center Leo Carlsson has been exceptionally active offensively throughout this playoff series, exceeding 3.5 shots on goal in all five games played. He has registered 26 shots on net in the series, significantly higher than his regular season average. As the Ducks play at home with a chance to close out the series, Carlsson is expected to maintain his aggressive shooting volume. Leo Carlsson is active and on the Anaheim Ducks roster for today's game.

The Atlanta Braves are a dominant force this season with a 22-9 record, leading MLB in runs scored and ranking second in OPS. They hold an impressive 8-game winning streak against the Tigers and have a 6-0 record in their last three seasons of head-to-head matchups. Braves' starter Bryce Elder boasts an excellent 1.95 ERA and 0.97 WHIP, significantly outperforming Tigers' starter Framber Valdez (3.41 ERA), especially against Atlanta's strong left-handed hitting lineup. The betting market has also shown a clear shift towards the Braves, with their moneyline moving from -118 to -131, indicating sharp money support.

Both the Diamondbacks and Brewers have demonstrated a strong tendency for high-scoring games this season, with the Over hitting in 17 of Arizona's 28 games and 16 of Milwaukee's 28 games. The head-to-head history between these two teams further supports this trend, with the Over cashing in 80% of their past matchups. While Zac Gallen is day-to-day for Arizona, scheduled starter Michael Soroka, despite a good ERA, rarely pitches deep into games, often exiting before the sixth inning. This, combined with key offensive players for both sides like Ildemaro Vargas for Arizona and Garrett Mitchell for Milwaukee, points to a game exceeding the total.

picks.game: Toronto Blue Jays vs Minnesota Twins
picks.rationale: The Minnesota Twins have shown a competitive edge against the Blue Jays recently, holding an 8-7 record in their last three seasons of head-to-head play and winning two of their three matchups this April. The Blue Jays are currently hampered by significant pitching injuries, including Max Scherzer on the 15-day IL and José Berríos just returning from a rehab outing. The Twins have also covered the spread in 48.4% of their games this season, indicating their ability to keep games close. These factors suggest the Twins are well-positioned to cover the +1.5 spread at home.

Both the Baltimore Orioles and Houston Astros have been involved in high-scoring contests recently, with the total runs going over in seven of the last eight Orioles matches and eight of the last ten Astros matches. Historically, the Astros have a strong offensive record against the Orioles, averaging 5.3 runs per game in their head-to-head matchups. The Orioles also have a setback with opening day starter Trevor Rodgers added to the injury list, which could impact their pitching performance. This combination of recent offensive trends and pitching concerns points towards a game with plenty of runs.

picks.game: St. Louis Cardinals vs Pittsburgh Pirates
picks.rationale: The St. Louis Cardinals have historically performed well against the Pittsburgh Pirates, holding a better overall head-to-head record and winning 14 of their last 26 matchups over the past three seasons. Despite being the moneyline underdog, the +1.5 spread at -110 odds offers excellent value, suggesting the Cardinals are expected to keep the game close. The Pirates' -245 moneyline indicates they are strong favorites, but the Cardinals' historical competitiveness in this rivalry makes the run line an attractive pick. This pick leverages the Cardinals' ability to stay within striking distance against their division rival.

Braga has demonstrated a strong offensive presence at home, scoring in 18 successive home matches. SC Freiburg has also been in potent scoring form in recent Europa League outings, netting 11 goals in their last three matches in the competition. Furthermore, Braga is dealing with several defensive injuries and a suspension, including Gabriel Moscardo, Diego Rodrigues, Sikou Niakate, and Adrian Leon Barisic, which could lead to a more open game. Given these factors, the odds of +125 for Over 2.5 goals present strong value for a high-scoring affair.

Nottingham Forest enters this match severely hampered by defensive injuries, with key center-back Murillo ruled out and Jair Cunha a major doubt, alongside other significant absences like Callum Hudson-Odoi for the season. Aston Villa, conversely, is in excellent form in the Europa League, having won their last five matches in the competition. While playing away, Aston Villa's robust European performance and Forest's critical defensive vulnerabilities provide a substantial advantage for the Villans to secure an away victory. The +160 odds offer compelling value given the disparity in team health and recent European form.

Both Aston Villa and Nottingham Forest have showcased strong offensive capabilities recently, each scoring 12 goals in their last five games in April. Nottingham Forest's significant defensive injury crisis, with Murillo ruled out and Jair Cunha a major doubt, is likely to compromise their ability to keep a clean sheet and could lead to a more expansive game. Their most recent head-to-head encounter, a 1-1 draw in April 2026, still featured a high volume of 28 shots with 11 on target, indicating offensive potential. Considering these attacking trends and Forest's defensive woes, the Over 2.5 at -102 is a high-confidence pick.

Braga holds a formidable home record in European competitions, having won 12 of their last 16 matches at their home stadium. In contrast, SC Freiburg has struggled significantly on the road, losing nine of their 16 Bundesliga matches and conceding 32 goals, and have been defeated in four of their last nine Europa League away games. Despite some defensive injuries for Braga, their strong home advantage and Freiburg's consistent struggles away from home position Braga as the favored side to win the first leg. The +125 odds for a home victory offer excellent value.
